title: Per-drug model artifact
description: The auditable JSON description of one trained drug model: the frozen feature vocabulary, the logistic-regression weights, the isotonic calibrator, the target gate, and the decision thresholds. A companion .joblib holds the live sklearn estimators; this JSON is the human-readable, diffable contract and is sufficient to reproduce a calibrated probability by hand.

feature_columns.description: Frozen, ordered feature vocabulary. Identical to (a subset of, in fixed order) the Module 1 feature_schema columns. Inference maps a new genome onto exactly these columns and never reshapes.

coef.description: One weight per entry in feature_columns, same order.

calibrator.description: Isotonic regression mapping raw model probability -> calibrated probability. Stored as its monotone knot points so the mapping is transparent and reproducible.

gate.description: Deterministic target gate. Overrides the model when the drug's molecular target is absent.

on_target_absent.description: 'susceptible' is intentionally excluded: an absent molecular target means the drug cannot act, so the gate must never resolve to 'likely to work' purely from target absence.

decision.description: How a calibrated probability becomes a call. A calibrated probability inside the no-call band around 0.5 (half-width = min_confidence - 0.5) is reported as no_call.

ood.description: Out-of-distribution guard. A query genome whose nearest training genome is farther than threshold is flagged and forced to no_call.

train_fingerprints.description: Presence/absence rows of the training genomes over feature_columns, used to compute the nearest-neighbour distance at inference time.
